Replace marketplace.json symlink with file copy (#312)

* Replace marketplace.json symlink with file copy

On Windows without developer mode, git checks out symlinks as plain text
files containing the target path. This caused Claude Code to fail parsing
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json since it contained the string
'../.github/plugin/marketplace.json' instead of valid JSON.

Replace the symlink with an actual copy of the file and update
CONTRIBUTING.md to instruct contributors to update both locations.

Fixes #300

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(cdn): orphan cleaner not cleaning step-log and service-log items (#7657)

Two issues prevented the orphan cleaner from cleaning up CDN items
whose workflow run no longer exists:

1. Stuck queue: the query ORDER BY created ASC LIMIT N always
   re-selected the same oldest non-orphan items, blocking newer
   orphaned items. Fixed by using a cursor-based pagination on the
   created timestamp, advancing through the full item set across
   successive batches.

2. Project-unaware check: ExistRunByID only checked the workflow_run
   table without verifying the parent project still exists. Orphaned
   workflow_run rows (left after project deletion, no CASCADE) were
   reported as existing. Fixed by adding a JOIN on the project table.

Signed-off-by: francois.samin <francois.samin@ovhcloud.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Defer hub.start() until HA is fully started

Use async_at_start() to start the gateway only after Home Assistant
has completed startup and all entities have been registered. This
fixes a race condition where status_callback could fire before
system sensors completed async_added_to_hass, causing the state
sensor to silently drop the Running update and stay stuck in
Initializing.

Fix mobile nav toggle on subpages via event delegation

The toggle listener previously used getElementById at script load time,
but subpage navs are injected asynchronously via fetch() — so the element
didn't exist yet and the listener was never attached.

Switch to document-level event delegation using closest(), which works
regardless of when the nav HTML is injected into the DOM.

commit.message:
fix: replace stale tokens (--moon/--faint/--muted) with design system tokens

Pages and components were using var(--moon), var(--faint), var(--muted)
which are undefined in the current design system — causing invisible text.

Replacements:
  var(--moon)  → var(--cream)  (primary body text)
  var(--faint) → var(--mist)   (secondary/muted text)
  var(--muted) → var(--mist)   (same)

Files fixed:
  app/(app)/report/page.tsx
  app/(app)/settings/profile/page.tsx
  components/report/DashboardReport.tsx
  components/insights/TodaysGuidance.tsx
  components/insights/DailyInsightCard.tsx

segment from entity_id with a regex targeting the _code_slot_N_ pattern.

contained numeric segments (e.g., "lock_2_front").

expected pattern.

Fix: replace platform-specific strftime format with portable alternative

Replace %-d (Linux-only strftime format) with .day attribute in f-string
for cross-platform compatibility. The %-d format specifier is not part of
the C standard and fails on Windows and some non-POSIX platforms.

Uses start.day which naturally produces unpadded day numbers on all
platforms.
